message.content: System Shutdown or Reboot (T1529) involves adversaries shutting down or rebooting systems to disrupt access or aid in system destruction. This technique can be executed via operating system commands or network device CLI commands. The impact includes significant disruption to legitimate user access and hastening the effects of other destructive actions like disk wiping or inhibiting system recovery. Common exploitation methods include malware that issues shutdown/reboot commands post-destruction, such as APT37's use of shutdown /r /t 1, APT38's BOOTWRECK, and HermeticWiper. Groups like Lazarus, LockerGoga, and Maze have also employed this technique to reboot systems after executing destructive payloads. Mitigation strategies include monitoring and alerting on shutdown/reboot commands, implementing robust backup and recovery processes, and ensuring proper access controls to prevent unauthorized command execution.
